claim: AI coding agents are best modeled as energetic but judgment-lacking junior developers; they execute fast and tireless but lack business context and make contextually-wrong-but-technically-correct code
actionable: When working with coding agents, retain decision-making for architectural choices and business context; delegate implementation details where the agent has clear specifications.
source_title: Agentic Engineering: Working With AI, Not Just Using It — Brendan O'Leary

claim: Context engineering is a critical bottleneck in agentic workflows; more context doesn't always mean better results—quality degrades around 50% context window fullness
actionable: Monitor context window usage; when approaching 50% fullness, summarize and compress prior work rather than accumulating more context; start new sessions to reset context.
source_title: Agentic Engineering: Working With AI, Not Just Using It — Brendan O'Leary

claim: Bad context corrupts agent output; if an agent is steered off course mid-session, residual negative context can cause wrong patterns to persist even after correction attempts
actionable: When realizing an agent is on the wrong path, start a new session rather than trying to correct in-place; have the previous agent summarize progress for handoff to avoid context corruption.
source_title: Agentic Engineering: Working With AI, Not Just Using It — Brendan O'Leary

claim: The research-plan-implement loop prevents errors; bad research leads to hundreds of lines of bad code; spend time understanding the problem before coding
actionable: Structure agent tasks as: (1) Research phase (ask-only mode, no file writes), (2) Plan phase (outline implementation steps, test strategy, scope boundaries), (3) Implement phase (execute with focused context).
source_title: Agentic Engineering: Working With AI, Not Just Using It — Brendan O'Leary

claim: Agent configuration should allow tuning autonomy: decide what the agent can do independently (run tests, read files) vs. what requires human approval; this balance must evolve as you learn
actionable: Start restrictive with agent autonomy (human approval for all changes); gradually increase autonomy for low-risk operations as you verify reliability.
source_title: Agentic Engineering: Working With AI, Not Just Using It — Brendan O'Leary

claim: agents.md is becoming the standard documentation for agent workflows; it should include conventions, build commands, testing requirements, and always-on rules for the project
actionable: Create an agents.md file in your repository documenting project conventions, testing commands, deployment procedures, and constraints; include it in all agent contexts.
source_title: Agentic Engineering: Working With AI, Not Just Using It — Brendan O'Leary

claim: Model Context Protocol (MCP) servers add system prompt overhead; each enabled MCP loads additional context tokens; disable MCPs you're not actively using to reduce noise
actionable: Audit enabled MCP servers before each agent session; disable any you're not using for the current task to avoid wasting tokens on irrelevant tools.
source_title: Agentic Engineering: Working With AI, Not Just Using It — Brendan O'Leary

claim: Frequent, small Git commits during agent work serve as first-pass code review; use Git locally before submitting PRs to understand and validate agent changes incrementally
actionable: During agent implementation, commit frequently to Git; review each commit as you would review a junior engineer's work before submitting for peer review.
source_title: Agentic Engineering: Working With AI, Not Just Using It — Brendan O'Leary
